text.title: Modular Monolith Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the modular-monolith methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Each module MUST map to exactly one bounded context. Modules named after technical layers (utils, helpers, common, lib) are not modules — they are coupling sinks and MUST be rejected.

rationale.source: DDD (Evans, Vernon); Shopify / GitHub modular-monolith case studies.

rule.rationale: Technical-layer modules accumulate cross-context logic and recreate the tight coupling modular monolith is meant to prevent.

rule.statement: Cross-module calls MUST go through a declared public API (typically `__public__.py`, `Module.API`, `exports`). Direct imports of another module's domain / application / infrastructure code are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bypassing public APIs creates invisible coupling; internal changes silently break callers and the boundary erodes within sprints.

rule.statement: Each module MUST own its own database schema (separate Postgres schema or table prefix). Cross-module reads MUST go through the public API, never via SQL JOINs.
rule.rationale: Schema-per-module is the prerequisite for future extraction; cross-module JOINs create the coupling extraction has to undo.

rule.statement: Boundary rules MUST be enforced by a static checker (import-linter, depguard, ArchUnit) in CI. Convention without enforcement decays within weeks.
rule.rationale: Human review misses boundary violations under deadline pressure; only mechanical enforcement preserves the architecture.

rule.statement: Cross-module communication for write events MUST use domain events on an in-process bus (or message queue), not shared tables. Shared tables imply shared ownership and re-create the big-ball-of-mud.
rule.rationale: Domain events keep modules autonomous; shared tables couple data lifecycles and complicate future extraction.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (bounded contexts named, dependency graph drawn, CI live, linter installed), apply r1 → r2 → r3 → r4 → r5 and emit the module spec.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order adoption (linter without dependency graph) catches noise; the order is gate-design.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) modules already need independent scaling, (b) polyglot stacks required, (c) throwaway prototype. Note constraint and exit.
rule.rationale: Modular ceremony for constraints it cannot satisfy wastes review time.
